question.text: What is the definition of 'stock broker' as per SEBI Stock Brokers Regulations, 1992?

answer.text: A person having trading rights in any recognised stock exchange and includes a trading member
Any person who provides investment advice to retail investors
A person who manages client portfolios for a fee
Any registered intermediary dealing in primary market issuances

explanation.text: **The Logic:** According to SEBI Stock Brokers Regulations, 1992, a stock broker means a person having trading rights in any recognised stock exchange and includes a trading member. **The Trap:** Investment advisers (option B) and portfolio managers (option C) are separate categories of intermediaries with their own regulations. Primary market dealing (option D) describes merchant bankers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Stock brokers execute buy/sell transactions in the secondary market on behalf of clients and earn brokerage commission. They may also help in marketing new issues.
